news.title: Substack launches a built-in recording studio
news.summary: Substack creators can pre-record video conversations with up to two guests, then publish directly on the platform.

news.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
news.summary: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.

news.title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
news.summary: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to re-engange its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

news.title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
news.summary: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

news.title: Lucid Motors shows off robotaxi concept called ‘Lunar’
news.summary: The concept was a highlight of Lucid's investor day, in which the company detailed its ambitions to push deeper into autonomy while pumping out new, affordable EVs.

news.title: Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
news.summary: Facebook Marketplace is adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. news.title: US Lawmakers Move to Kill the FBI’s Warrantless Wiretap Access
news.summary: A bipartisan bill would force the FBI to get a warrant to read Americans’ messages and ban the federal purchase of commercial data on US residents ahead of a critical April deadline.
